title: erysimum cheiranthus subsp. ochroleucum
intro: Erysimum cheiranthus subsp. ochroleucum, also known as the Creamy Wallflower, is a perennial herb in the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and lance-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Erysimum cheiranthus subsp. ochroleucum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to alleviate colds, flu, and other respiratory conditions.
